records.ety: [AS. drencan to give to drink, to drench, the causal of drincan to drink; akin to D. drenken, Sw. dränka, G. tränken. See Drink.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Drenched (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Drenching.]

defs: To cause to drink; especially, to dose by force; to put a potion down the throat of, as of a horse; hence. to purge violently by physic.
quotes: As “to fell,” is “to make to fall,” and “to lay,” to make to lie.” so “to drench,” is “to make to drink.”

defs: To steep in moisture; to wet thoroughly; to soak; to saturate with water or other liquid; to immerse.
quotes: Now dam the ditches and the floods restrain; Their moisture has already drenched the plain.

defs: A drink; a draught; specifically, a potion of medicine poured or forced down the throat; also, a potion that causes purging.
quotes: Give my roan horse a drench.

records.ety: [AS. dreng warrior, soldier, akin to Icel. drengr.]

defs: A military vassal mentioned in Domesday Book.
